critical_insights.primary_issue: Solution file (RepoLens.sln) incorrectly placed at repository root for multi-technology project
current_problems: Solution file at root implies .NET-only project
Mixed technology concerns at repository level
Build artifacts (bin/, obj/) scattered in source structure
Tests not organized by technology stack
No clear README explaining multi-tech architecture

professional_restructuring_operations.rationale: Solution files belong with their technology ecosystem (.NET)
professional_restructuring_operations.impact: MAJOR - CI/CD pipelines must be updated
professional_restructuring_operations.enterprise_justification: Multi-tech repositories separate solutions by technology

professional_restructuring_operations.rationale: Build artifacts should never be in source control
Tests should be organized by technology first, then by type

items.content:
# RepoLens

## Multi-Technology Repository

This repository contains:
- **Backend**: .NET 8 API and Services (`src/backend/`)
- **Frontend**: React TypeScript Application (`src/frontend/`)
- **Tests**: Technology-separated testing (`tests/`)

## Quick Start

### Backend (.NET)
```bash
cd src/backend
dotnet restore
dotnet build
```

### Frontend (React)
```bash
cd src/frontend/repolens-ui
npm install
npm start
```

## Architecture

See `docs/architecture/` for detailed system design.

professional_restructuring_operations.rationale: Prevent build artifacts and dependencies from entering source control

expected_final_structure.technology_separation: Complete - each technology has its own ecosystem

enterprise_benefits: Clear technology separation for multi-tech teams
Backend and frontend can be developed independently
Reduced confusion about project scope and architecture
Build artifacts properly excluded from source control
migration_impact: MAJOR - Requires team communication and CI/CD updates
